title: Rental Deposit Assistance - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Housing & Shelter > Rental Deposit Assistance 77 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter Agricultural Laborers AIDS/HIV Families with Children Foster Children Homeless Veterans Homeless Youth Homelessness Issues LGBTQ2+ Individuals Medicaid Recipients Native American Community * Homelessness Issues Older Adults Pregnant Individuals Slavic Community Veterans Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness ACCESS RENT DEPOSIT ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Helps pay for refundable deposits to get into affordable housing. Service is limited and depends on availability of funding. 3630 Aviation Way Medford, OR 97504 Eligibility: Varies Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-5pm, Friday 8am-4:30pm Email (541) 414-0318 0 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ACCESS SUPPORTIVE SERVICES FOR VETERAN FAMILIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a short-term housing intervention program with assistance through case management. Support may include move-in costs, rental assistance, eviction prevention, benefits support, employment and education services, and legal aid around housing barriers. Must be willing to work with a caseworker. May require clients to be alcohol-and drug-free. Assistance depends on the availability of funding and eligibility. 3630 Aviation Way Medford, OR 97504 Eligibility: -Veterans and veteran families who are homeless or at imminent risk of homelessness -Veteran must be present in household -Any discharge other than dishonorable Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-4:30pm Email (458) 488-1340 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ADVANCED HEALTH FLEXIBLE SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Flexible Services or "flex funds," are non-covered services offered in addition to the covered benefits of the Oregon Health Plan (OHP) through Coordinated Care Organizations (CCOs) to improve care delivery and overall member health and well-being. Eligibility for services is determined by the CCO. Services can include but are not limited to: -Housing support (rent assistance, rental deposit assistance [requires move-in date], utility bills, housing programs for women and children) -Temporary motel stays (For individuals being discharged from the hospital; experiencing an extreme weather event; has a set move-in date for a new home and needs a temporary place to stay before then; or actively fleeing a domestic violence situation.) -Electronics such as cellphones, computers, and other communication devices -Specific educational materials and/or classes -Family and home resources (ex. items for child health and safety, parenting programs, household appliances, climate devices) -Gym memberships and exercise classes/equipment -Health and substance use prevention support All flexible services are determined on a case-by-case basis by the CCO. HRS are NOT intended to address emergency situations. CCOs are not required to offer flexible services. As flexible services are not a covered benefit, a denial cannot be appealed, but an OHP member can submit a complaint. Eligibility: -Must be a current member of the CCO -Member must be unable to receive requested service by any other benefit or means -Item or service requested must help with medical need or life situation -All documentation requested for service must be provided Health-related services are limited, not immediate, not guaranteed or continuously available, and individuals may not qualify. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (888) 460-0185 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BLUE MOUNTAIN ACTION COUNCIL SSVF SUPPORTIVE SERVICES FOR VETERAN FAMILIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a short-term housing intervention program for low-income veterans and their families experiencing homelessness or at imminent risk of becoming homeless within 30 days or less. Veterans and their families can be assisted with housing through case management, pending eligibility. If eligible, assistance and support may include move-in/rental assistance, eviction prevention, benefits support, employment and education services, legal aid around housing barriers, and other expenses that may support stable housing. Must be willing to work with a caseworker. Assistance in any of these programs depends on availability of funds. 312 N 2nd Avenue Walla Walla, WA 99362 Eligibility: Veterans and veteran families who are homeless or at imminent risk of homelessness with any discharge other than "Dishonorable." Veteran must be present in household. Hours: Monday-Thursday 8:30am-5pm, Friday 8:30am-4pm Email (509) 529-4980 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BROOKINGS CORE RESPONSE PEER HOUSE NAVIGATION CENTER RAPID REHOUSING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Registration and referral for Oregon Health Plan's Health Related Social Needs program. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 883-2947 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTRAL OREGON VETERANS OUTREACH SUPPORTIVE SERVICES FOR VETERAN FAMILIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a short-term housing intervention program for low-income veterans and their families experiencing homelessness or at imminent risk of becoming homeless. Eligible veterans and their families can be supported in obtaining housing or avoiding eviction in their current housing. Case management support may include move-in/rental assistance, eviction prevention (rental and utility arrears), benefits support, employment and education services, legal aid around housing barriers, and other expenses that may support stable housing. All temporary financial assistance is subject to the Veteran's eligibility according to SSVF/VA program policies. Financial assistance depends on availability of funds. 61510 S Highway 97 Suite 100 Bend, OR 977023706 Eligibility: Veterans and veteran families who are homeless or at imminent risk of homelessness and have an income at or below 80% of Area Median Income with any discharge other than "Dishonorable." Veteran must be present in household and if housed on the lease. Members of a household are determined by the Veteran. Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm Email (541) 383-2793 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLATSOP COMMUNITY ACTION RENTAL ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides several forms of housing and rental assistance: -Housing Stabilization Program (HSP): one time rental or deposit assistance for families -Emergency Solutions Grant (ESG): one time rental assistance or rental deposit assistance -Continuum of Care (CoC): 2 transitional apartment rental units and 4 Federal housing programs assisting unsheltered youth age 18-24, DV survivors, and chronically homeless families -Emergency Housing Assistance (EHA) One time rental or deposit assistance for families -Elderly Rental Assistance (ERA) One time rental or deposit assistance for individuals age 58 or over 364 9th Street Astoria, OR 97103 Eligibility: Varies by program Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-3:30pm Email (503) 325-1400 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COLUMBIA PACIFIC CCO FLEXIBLE SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Flexible Services or "flex funds," are non-covered services offered in addition to the covered benefits of the Oregon Health Plan (OHP) through Coordinated Care Organizations (CCOs) to improve care delivery and overall member health and well-being. Eligibility for services is determined by the CCO. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Email (503) 416-4100 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY ACTION PROGRAM OF EAST CENTRAL OREGON MAIN OFFICE HELPING HAND Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides emergency financial assistance to address basic needs, including emergency lodging for up to three nights, prescription assistance for non-narcotic medications (twice per 12-month period), auto repairs up to $200 (once per 12-month period), laundry vouchers up to $10 per individual and $20 per family (twice per 12-month period), and rent payment or deposit assistance up to $300, but not more than 70% of one month's rent (once per 18-month period). 211 SE Byers Avenue Pendleton, OR 97801 Eligibility: -Laundry vouchers: Unrestricted -Prescription assistance: Must have a written or phoned-in prescription from Walmart or Safeway -Rent assistance: Must be facing immediate eviction, potential homelessness, or experiencing homelessness -Lodging: Must be transitioning to permanent housing -Auto repairs: Must be in need of transportation to employment, medical appointment, or school Hours: Tuesday-Friday 9am-11:30am by appointment only (541) 276-3418 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY ACTION PROGRAM OF EAST CENTRAL OREGON MAIN OFFICE SUPPORTIVE SERVICES FOR VETERAN FAMILIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a short-term housing intervention program for veterans and their families.Veterans and their families can be assisted with housing through case management, pending eligibility. If eligible, assistance and support may include move-in/rental assistance, eviction prevention, benefits support, employment and education services, legal aid around housing barriers, and other expenses that may support stable housing. Must be willing to work with a caseworker. May require clients to be alcohol- and drug-free. Assistance in any of these programs depends on availability of funds. 211 SE Byers Avenue Pendleton, OR 97801 Eligibility: Veterans and veteran families who are homeless or at imminent risk of homelessness and have an income at or below 50% of Area Median Income with any discharge other than "Dishonorable." Veteran must be present in household. Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-5pm, Friday 8am-noon (541) 276-1926 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY ACTION TEAM SUPPORTIVE SERVICES FOR VETERAN FAMILIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers short-term housing assistance. Support may include help with finding housing, move-in or rental assistance, eviction prevention, and access to benefits, employment, education, legal services related to housing barriers, and other expenses that support stable housing. Case management is available, pending eligibility. Participation requires working with a caseworker, and some programs may require individuals to be drug- and alcohol-free. All assistance is based on eligibility and the availability of funding. 125 N 17th Street Saint Helens, OR 97051 Eligibility: Veterans and their families who are homeless or at risk of becoming homeless and have an income at or below 80% of Area Median Income with any discharge other than Dishonorable or General Court Martial. Veteran must be present in the household. Not suitable for emergencies as the process may take several weeks or more. Assistance is based on available funding and is not guaranteed. 1440 Birch Avenue Cottage Grove, OR 97424 Eligibility: Individuals who self-identify as low income Hours: Monday 1:30pm-6pm, Tuesday-Friday 11am-3:30pm Email (541) 942-2176 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CONFEDERATED TRIBES OF THE COOS, LOWER UMPQUA, AND SIUSLAW INDIANS GOVERNMENT OFFICE EMERGENCY ASSISTANCE/HOMELESS PREVENTION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides transitional housing rental assistance or a rental deposit, which may include placement in community-based crisis centers, transitional housing, or motels, or payment of one month of rent or a deposit toward a new rental unit. Assistance is based on funding availability and is not guaranteed. 1245 Fulton Avenue Coos Bay, OR 97420 Eligibility: Must be enrolled in a federally recognized tribe, and families that are experiencing homelessness. Homelessness that was not caused by eviction for drug or alcohol issues, criminal activity, or any other reason due to the fault of the family, excluding non-payment of rent. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 888-1310 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EASTERN OREGON COORDINATED CARE ORGANIZATION FLEXIBLE SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Flexible Services or "flex funds," are non-covered services offered in addition to the covered benefits of the Oregon Health Plan (OHP) through Coordinated Care Organizations (CCOs) to improve care delivery and overall member health and well-being. Eligibility for services is determined by the CCO. Services can include but are not limited to: -Housing support (rent assistance, rental deposit assistance [requires move-in date], utility bills, housing programs for women and children) -Temporary motel stays (for individuals being discharged from the hospital or who have a set move-in date for a new home and need a temporary place to stay before then) -Electronics such as cell phones, computers, and other communication devices when there are no state programs available in the area -Family and home resources (ex. items for child health and safety, parenting programs, household appliances, climate devices) -Gym memberships or home equipment -Beds and mattresses for children under 21 or for adults with a diagnosis of a back or spine condition -Car seats if there are no other county or programs available in the area All flexible services are determined on a case-by-case basis by the CCO. HRS are NOT intended to address emergency situations. CCOs are not required to offer flexible services. As flexible services are not a covered benefit, a denial cannot be appealed, but an OHP member can submit a complaint. Eligibility: -Must be a current member of the CCO -Member must be unable to receive requested service by any other benefit or means -Item or service requested must help with medical need or life situation -All documentation requested for service must be provided Health-related services are limited, not immediate, not guaranteed or continuously available, and individuals may not qualify. Visit website to review additional eligibility criteria regarding specific programs and benefits. Hours: Monday-Friday 7:30am-5:30pm Email (503) 765-3521 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EASTERSEALS OREGON EASTERSEALS OREGON SALEM SUPPORTIVE SERVICES FOR VETERAN FAMILIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a short-term housing intervention program for low-income veterans and their families experiencing homelessness or at imminent risk of becoming homeless. Veterans and their families can be assisted with housing through case management, pending eligibility. If eligible, assistance and support may include move-in/rental assistance, eviction prevention, benefits support, employment and education services, legal aid around housing barriers, and other expenses that may support stable housing. Must be willing to work with a caseworker. Assistance in any of these programs depends on availability of funds. 3878 Beverly Avenue NE Building H, Suite 5 Salem, OR 97305 Eligibility: Veterans and veteran families that are homeless or at imminent risk of homelessness and have an income at or below 80% of Area Median Income with any discharge other than "Dishonorable." Veteran must be present in household to participate. As flexible services are not a covered benefit, a denial cannot be appealed, but an OHP member can submit a complaint. 500 Summer Street NE E-49 Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: -Must be a current member of the CCO -Member must be unable to receive requested service by any other benefit or means -Item or service requested must help with medical need or life situation -All documentation requested for service must be provided Health-related services are limited, not immediate, not guaranteed or continuously available, and individuals may not qualify. Depending on individual needs, assistance may also cover items like medical supplies, storage fees, camping supplies, or furniture. All assistance depends on current funding and is not guaranteed. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: No standard hours; calls are returned during regular business hours Monday-Friday 9am-4pm Email (541) 708-5650 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TRANSITION PROJECTS VETERAN SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a short-term housing intervention program for low-income veterans and their families experiencing homelessness or at imminent risk of becoming homeless. Veterans and their families can be assisted with housing through case management, pending eligibility. If eligible, assistance and support may include move-in costs and rental assistance, eviction prevention, benefits support, employment and education services, legal aid around housing barriers, and other expenses that may support stable housing. Must be willing to work with a caseworker. Assistance in any of these programs depends on availability of funds. 7916 SE Foster Road Portland, OR 97206 Eligibility: Veterans and their households, who are homeless or at imminent risk of homelessness, and have an income at or below 80% of Area Median Income. Households are only eligible if the veteran applies and is eligible.
